prs.title: Back-end ended
prs.body:
# Full-Stack JWT Authentication System

## Overview
This PR implements a complete authentication system with **JWT-based security**, connecting a **Node.js/Express backend** with a **React (Vite) frontend**. The architecture emphasizes backend security, database management with Prisma ORM, and seamless API integration.

---

## 🏗️ Backend Architecture (Focus)

### Tech Stack
- **Runtime**: Node.js (ES Modules)
- **Framework**: Express.js v5
- **Database**: MySQL with Prisma ORM
- **Authentication**: JWT (jsonwebtoken)
- **Security**: bcrypt for password hashing
- **CORS**: Enabled for cross-origin requests

### Project Structure
backend/
├── src/
│ ├── controllers/
│ │ └── authController.js # Business logic for auth
│ ├── routes/
│ │ └── authRoutes.js # API route definitions
│ ├── utils/
│ │ ├── hashPassword.js # Password hashing utilities
│ │ └── token.js # JWT generation & verification
│ ├── db/
│ │ └── prisma.js # Prisma client instance
│ ├── index.js # Express app configuration
│ └── server.js # Server initialization
├── prisma/
│ └── schema.prisma # Database schema
└── .env # Environment variables


---

## 🔑 Core Backend Components

### 1. **Database Schema (Prisma)**
```prisma
model User {
  id       Int    @id @default(autoincrement())
  name     String
  email    String @unique
  password String
}

Copy

Insert at cursor
MySQL database with Prisma ORM

User model with unique email constraint

Auto-incrementing primary key

2. Password Security
File: utils/hashPassword.js

Uses bcrypt with salt rounds = 10

hashPass(): Hashes plain passwords before storage

verifyPass(): Compares plain password with hashed version during login

3. JWT Token Management
File: utils/token.js

generateToken(): Creates JWT with userId payload

Token expiry: 7 days

Secret stored in environment variable (JWT_SECRET)

verifyTokens(): Validates JWT (currently unused but available)

4. Authentication Controller
File: controllers/authController.js

Signup Flow (POST /api/signup)
Validates required fields (name, email, password)

Checks if user already exists

Hashes password with bcrypt

Creates user in database via Prisma

Generates JWT token

Returns token to client

Login Flow (POST /api/login)
Validates email and password

Finds user by email

Verifies password using bcrypt

Generates JWT token

Returns token to client

5. API Routes
File: routes/authRoutes.js

POST /api/signup → signupUser

POST /api/login → loginUser

File: index.js

GET /api/users → Fetch all users (protected route)

GET / → Health check endpoint

6. Middleware Configuration
app.use(cors());           // Enable cross-origin requests
app.use(express.json());   // Parse JSON request bodies

Copy

Insert at cursor
🌐 Frontend Integration
Tech Stack
Framework: React 18 with Vite

HTTP Client: Axios

State Management: React Hooks (useState, useEffect)

Storage: localStorage for JWT persistence

API Communication
Signup Request
POST http://localhost:3001/api/signup
Body: { name, email, password }
Response: { message, token }

Copy

Insert at cursor
javascript
Login Request
POST http://localhost:3001/api/login
Body: { email, password }
Response: { message, token }

Copy

Insert at cursor
javascript
Fetch Users (Protected)
GET http://localhost:3001/api/users
Response: [{ id, name, email }]

Copy

Insert at cursor
javascript
Authentication Flow
User submits signup/login form

Frontend sends credentials to backend API

Backend validates, hashes password, generates JWT

Frontend stores JWT in localStorage

Token persists across page refreshes

Protected routes use token for authorization

Logout clears token from storage

🔄 Request-Response Cycle
┌─────────────┐                    ┌─────────────┐
│   React     │                    │   Express   │
│  Frontend   │                    │   Backend   │
└──────┬──────┘                    └──────┬──────┘
       │                                  │
       │  POST /api/signup                │
       │  { name, email, password }       │
       ├─────────────────────────────────>│
       │                                  │
       │                           Check existing user
       │                           Hash password (bcrypt)
       │                           Save to MySQL (Prisma)
       │                           Generate JWT
       │                                  │
       │  { message, token }              │
       │<─────────────────────────────────┤
       │                                  │
   Store token                            │
   in localStorage                        │
       │                                  │
       │  GET /api/users                  │
       ├─────────────────────────────────>│
       │                                  │
       │                           Fetch from database
       │                                  │
       │  [{ id, name, email }]           │
       │<─────────────────────────────────┤
       │                                  │


Copy

Insert at cursor
🚀 Running the Application
Backend
cd backend
npm install
npm run dev  # Runs on http://localhost:3001

Copy

Insert at cursor
bash
Frontend
cd frontend/vite
npm install
npm run dev  # Runs on http://localhost:5173

Copy

Insert at cursor
bash
🔒 Security Features
✅ Password Hashing: bcrypt with salt rounds
✅ JWT Authentication: Secure token-based auth
✅ Email Uniqueness: Database constraint prevents duplicates
✅ Input Validation: Required field checks
✅ Error Handling: Proper HTTP status codes
✅ CORS Configuration: Controlled cross-origin access

📝 Environment Variables
DATABASE_URL="mysql://user:password@localhost:3306/dbname"
JWT_SECRET="your-secret-key"
PORT=3001

Copy

Insert at cursor
env
🎯 Key Takeaways
Modular Backend: Separation of concerns (routes, controllers, utils)

Prisma ORM: Type-safe database queries

Stateless Auth: JWT eliminates server-side session storage

RESTful API: Clean endpoint design

Frontend Simplicity: No router needed, conditional rendering only

prs.title: Footer - ready
prs.body:
##  **Pull Request: Update Footer and Social Links**

Related issues - #4477 

### **Title**

**feat: Update footer and social links**

---

### **Summary**

This PR updates and improves the footer section and associated social link components for better styling, functionality, and consistency across the homepage.

---

### **Changes Made**

- Installed and implemented Tailwindcss
* **Restyled Footer (`Footer.jsx`):**

  * Added rounded corners for a smoother, modern appearance.
  * Adjusted layout and spacing for improved visual hierarchy.

* **Updated Footer Links:**

  * Ensured all footer links direct to the correct sections or pages.
  * Improved link accessibility and hover states.

* **Fixed and Enhanced Social Icons (`SocialLinks.jsx` & `socialLinksList.js`):**

  * Restored missing social icons.
  * Fixed hover effects and ensured consistent visual feedback.
  * Updated social links configuration in `socialLinksList.js`.

* **Minor Integration Updates (`News.jsx`):**

  * Ensured footer and social links render correctly within the News section.
  * Verified alignment and responsiveness across different breakpoints.

---

### **Files Modified**

* `src/Home/Footer.jsx`
* `src/Home/News.jsx`
* `src/components/SocialLinks.jsx`
* `src/config/socialLinksList.js`
* etc


---

### **Testing & Validation**

* ✅ Verified that all footer links navigate to correct destinations.
* ✅ Confirmed all social icons appear and hover states function properly.
* ✅ Checked layout responsiveness on various screen sizes.
* ✅ Ensured no console or build errors after changes.

---

### **Notes**

* This PR disregards the invalid `-p` flag previously used during commit staging.
* Entire `src` directory was staged to ensure all relevant updates were included.
* Further design tweaks can be made in a follow-up PR if needed.

prs.title: Navbar Visual enhanacement
prs.body:
The “Explore” and “Community” dropdowns have been redesigned for a cleaner, more seamless look. They now extend smoothly from the navbar with matching background, aligned borders, and rounded bottom corners—removing overlap and creating a cohesive, professional navigation experience.


Category | Details
-- | --
Description | Improved the “Explore” and “Community” dropdowns for a seamless, aligned look with the navbar. Removed overlap and refined borders for a cleaner design.
Key Changes | Dropdowns now extend smoothly from the navbar, share background and blur, and have only bottom corners rounded.
Impact | Cleaner, consistent, and more professional navigation experience.
Testing | Hover over “Explore” and “Community” to confirm dropdown alignment, smooth transition, and proper border styling.

Closes #479

prs.title: Project smooth scroll
prs.body:
### Description

Fixes inconsistent scrolling on the Projects page by integrating `lenis` for smooth scrolling and adds a user-configurable toggle to enable or disable smooth scrolling across the app.

### Related Issues

Fixes #463

### Motivation

Enhances UX by standardizing scroll behavior and giving users control over smooth scrolling. While `gsap` handles animations, it doesn’t provide global smooth scrolling.

### Changes Made

* **`src/components/Projects.jsx`**:

  * Imported and called `useLenis()` to enable smooth scrolling.
* **`/SettingsMenu.jsx` & `SettingsContext`**:

  * Added `smoothScroll` setting and toggle in the settings menu.
  * Updated `useLenis` hook to activate `lenis` conditionally based on user preference.
  * Removed redundant hardcoded "Smooth Scroll" entry to fix duplicate toggle issue.

### Impact

* Smooth, consistent scrolling on Projects page.
* User can now enable/disable smooth scrolling app-wide.
* Aligns scroll implementation with project standards.

### Testing

1. Navigate to Projects page and other pages with smooth scrolling.
2. Verify scrolling behavior matches Landing/Events pages.
3. Toggle "Smooth Scroll" in SettingsMenu and ensure scrolling activates/deactivates accordingly.
4. Confirm no duplicate "Smooth Scroll" toggle exists.

prs.title: Feature/trap focus modal bottomsheet
prs.body: ## Description - Added a custom focus trap to the Modal component. - Ensures `Tab` and `Shift+Tab` navigation stays within the modal. - Automatically focuses the first element on open (optional). - Removed the need for `focus-trap-react`, improving control and reducing dependencies. - Scalable and reusable across other components like BottomSheet. ## Type of Change - [x] ✨ New feature - [x] ♻️ Code refactor ## Related Issues Fixes #222 [Issue Link](https://github.com/Newton-School/grauity/issues/222) ## Checklist - [x] Clear PR title - [x] Code self-reviewed - [x] No unnecessary eslint disables - [x] No new TypeScript warnings - [x] Clean history ## Additional Notes This improves accessibility and aligns with common dialog behavior patterns. ## Screenshots (implemented code)

issues.title: [FEATURE] Interactive Widgets with Heatmap & Direct Habit Access
issues.body:
**Is your feature request related to a problem? Please describe.**
Currently, the widgets in Habo are quite limited in terms of interactivity and information density. They don’t provide a quick visual overview of progress (like streak patterns), nor do they allow users to directly jump into a specific habit from the widget. This makes it harder to quickly check progress or take action without opening the app and navigating manually.

**Describe the solution you'd like**
I’d love to see more advanced and interactive widgets, inspired by apps like HabitNow or Habit Tracker HabitBull. Specifically:

* A **heatmap-style widget** showing streak/activity over time (similar to GitHub contribution graphs)
* Ability to **select a specific habit** to display in the widget
* **Tap interactions**:

  * Tap on the widget → opens that specific habit’s detail page
  * Tap on a day → opens that habit filtered to that date (if feasible)
* Optional **compact and expanded widget sizes**
* Visual indicators for:

  * current streak
  * completion status today
  * missed days

This would make widgets far more useful as a daily touchpoint rather than just passive displays.

**Describe alternatives you've considered**
Currently, the only way to view detailed streak progress or focus on a single habit is to open the app and navigate manually. Other apps like Habitify and Loop Habit Tracker offer better at-a-glance insights, but they still lack deeply interactive widget experiences. A widget-based solution inside Habo would be more seamless.

**Additional context**
This feature would:

* Improve daily engagement
* Reduce friction for checking habits
* Make Habo feel more “alive” on the home screen

Even a first version with a simple heatmap + tap-to-open-habit would already be a huge improvement.

Thanks for the amazing app—this would be a big usability upgrade!

issues.title: Minor UI Navbar Alignement Fix
issues.body:
### Current Behavior
When hovering between two adjacent navbar dropdowns (e.g., “Resources” → “Community”), the dropdown menus briefly overlap or flicker for around 2 seconds before stabilizing.
This creates a small but visible UI glitch in the navbar hover animation.
(Check the attached screen recoding)

### Desired Behavior
Hover transitions between navbar dropdowns should be smooth, with no overlap or flicker between dropdown menus.

### Screenshots/Logs
https://github.com/user-attachments/assets/21d2037a-f791-4abb-866d-9bb4515d6240

### Environment

- **Browser(s):** Arc Browser, Chrome 130.0.6723.91, Safari 18.0, Firefox 131.0
- **Host OS:** macOS Sonoma 15.0.1 (also tested on Windows 11)
- **Meshery Environment:** Website UI (meshery.io)


---

### Contributor [Guides](https://docs.meshery.io/project/contributing) and [Handbook](https://meshery.io/community#handbook)

issues.title: Hosting the Database on render so that .env prisma configuration become smoother for everyone for database
[ENHANCEMENT] Improving Navbar Dropdown
issues.body: ## Navbar Dropdown Overlap Issue **Current Behavior** The dropdown menus for the **Explore** and **Community** buttons are overlapping the existing navbar, causing a cluttered and visually broken layout. The dropdown appears on top of other navbar elements instead of below them, making it look unpolished and hard to use. **Proposed Enhancement** Update the navbar dropdowns to appear **directly below** their respective buttons with proper spacing. Ensure that: * The dropdown does not overlap other navbar elements. * The dropdown has clear visual separation (e.g., background, shadow, or border). * Proper `z-index` and positioning are applied so it behaves consistently across screen sizes. **Expected Outcome** * Dropdowns display **below** the buttons in a visually clean and organized manner. * Navbar remains fully visible and uncluttered when dropdowns are open. * Improved usability and polished visual presentation across devices. **Additional Context** * Could use `absolute` positioning relative to the navbar buttons and proper `z-index` layering. * Ensure responsiveness for mobile and tablet layouts. * Reference design systems or examples where dropdowns appear cleanly below the navbar. * Optional: Add subtle shadow or animation for better UX.

issues.title: [FEATURE]  Improve and Modernize Footer Design
issues.body:
### Is your feature request related to a problem? Please describe.

Currently, the footer on ZettaNote feels minimal and lacks professional polish. It doesn’t provide key information or design consistency with the rest of the site’s interface.

### Describe the solution you'd like

I would like to introduce a redesigned, more professional footer that includes:

Clear branding (logo, name, tagline)
Quick navigation links (Home, About, Docs, Contact, etc.)
Social media and GitHub repository links
Copyright notice and year (automatically updated)
Subtle styling (consistent color scheme, spacing, and typography)
This will enhance the overall user experience and visual consistency across the site.

### Describe alternatives you've considered

_No response_

### Additional context

A new footer could also include a light/dark theme adaptation to maintain consistency across themes.

issues.title: [FEATURE] cmd+k shortcut command search
issues.body:
### Problem / Motivation | Issue | Impact | |---------------------------------|-------------------------------------| | No quick keyboard shortcut for search or command palette | - Slower user navigation
- Reduced productivity
- Less intuitive access to search | ### Proposed Solution - Add a global keyboard shortcut: **Cmd+K** (macOS) / **Ctrl+K** (Windows/Linux) - Shortcut triggers opening or focusing the search input or command palette - Ensure the shortcut overrides default browser behavior when necessary ### Alternatives considered | Alternative | Reason not preferred | |------------------------------|------------------------------------| | Clicking search icon manually | Slower and less convenient | | Other keyboard shortcuts | Less standardized or discoverable | --- ## Acceptance Criteria | Criteria | Description | |---------------------------------------------------------|-------------------------------------------------| | Shortcut functionality | Cmd+K / Ctrl+K opens or focuses the search UI | | Cross-browser and platform consistency | Works reliably on major browsers and OS | | Prevent default browser shortcut behavior | Default Cmd+K action (e.g., focus browser bar) is suppressed when used in the app |

issues.title: Improve Landing Page UI/UX to Better Reflect Pollinations' Creative Capabilities
issues.body:
# Motivation

The current landing page UI/UX feels cluttered, not well structured and does not fully communicate the innovative, creative potential of Pollinations.ai’s generative AI platform.
Enhancing the visual design and user experience will make it easier for visitors to understand the product offerings, build trust, and increase engagement with the platform.

This improvement will especially benefit new users who discover Pollinations for the first time by giving them a clearer and more inspiring introduction.

# Scope
- Redesign the landing page layout to better highlight core features such as AI-generated images, avatars, and APIs.
- Improve typography, color schemes, and spacing for better readability and visual hierarchy.
- Add interactive or animated elements to showcase generative AI capabilities in a subtle but engaging way.
- Include clear call-to-action (CTA) buttons for users to quickly start generating content or explore APIs.
- Ensure responsive design for all device sizes.
- Keep improvements focused on the landing page only to keep scope manageable.

# Options
- Create a static mockup using tools Figma  to visualize the redesign before implementation.
- Implement using the existing frontend framework used by Pollinations.ai.
- Use lightweight animations or SVG visuals to demonstrate generative capabilities without harming performance.
- Gather feedback from the community or maintainers to iterate on the design before final implementation.

# Related
- None currently, but could link to any existing frontend or UX improvement issues if found.

issues.title: Enhancement: Landing Page UI/UX, Alignments, Visuals & Content Expansion
issues.body: ## Overview The current ZettaNote landing page is functional but unclear & too basic. It lacks key sections and visual appeal that help build trust and showcase the app’s value. ## Problem - Landing page is short (about 1.5 scrolls) - Missing footer, social proof(can skip for now), and detailed visuals - Needs improvements to build credibility and look more professional ## Proposed Enhancements ### 1. Content Additions - **Footer:** - At the bottom - Links: Home, Features, GitHub, Privacy Policy, Terms of Service (These are essentials and must be provided on how the data is handled). - Copyright © 2025 ZettaNote - **Social Proof:** (Can skip for now as its in initial phase) - Between “Why Choose ZettaNote?” and final CTA - Show user quotes, tweets, or stats (e.g., GitHub stars) - **FAQ Section:** - Before final CTA - Accordion style - Answer common questions (e.g., free usage, self-hosting) ### 2. UI/UX Improvements - **Visuals in Features Section:** - Add screenshots, icons, or GIFs for each feature - **CTA Buttons:** - Hero and final CTA - Show “Get Started for Free” or “Sign Up” for new/guest users - Show “GO TO DASHBOARD” for logged-in users - **Features Grid Layout:** - Fix asymmetry in 5-item grid - Add 6th feature or redesign grid for balance ### 3. Technical & Accessibility - **SEO & Social Sharing:** - Add meta title and description tags - Add Open Graph tags for social preview - **Accessibility:** - Keyboard navigable elements - Alt text for images/icons - WCAG-compliant color contrast
